dropped.reason: too short to carry a claim

meta.model: Gemini gemini-flash-lite-latest · US (and trains on free-tier prompts)

clusters.label: iPad cursor jumping and text input failures
Forced AI features cluttering the interface
Severe mobile app lag, freezes, and crashes
Lack of Ukrainian and other local language interfaces
Disappearing notes and severe data loss

insights.title: Progressive performance degradation causes persistent lagging, freezing, and forced app restarts during editing sessions.
insights.summary: Mobile users experience significant slowdowns, interface unresponsiveness, and unexpected crashes when editing documents or managing databases. Many report having to force-close and relaunch the application multiple times during a single session to restore basic interactivity. These stability issues disproportionately impact users handling larger pages or trying to perform rapid offline capture. As performance continues to decline with successive updates, customer trust is eroded.

evidence.quote: The worst part about this app is that just when you start depending on it for something important it starts glitching in some new and horribly frustrating way. Don’t waste your…

evidence.quote: Consistently has been one of the most laggy and buggy apps on my phone. Lots of features, but the core functionality of just editing a file takes so long as nothing seems to be…

evidence.quote: i’ll be typing in a page and then all of a sudden it won’t let me type or paste anything. i go back (it won’t even let me hit the back button, I have to swipe back) and try to…

insights.recommendation: Optimize database rendering and local caching routines to eliminate UI freezes and reduce memory overhead on mobile devices.

insights.title: Forced and unremovable AI prompt bars obscure navigation controls and frustrate users who prefer a clean interface.
insights.summary: Recent updates have replaced traditional navigation shortcuts, such as the home and favorite buttons, with prominent generative assistant bars at the bottom of the screen. Users report that these persistent elements take up valuable screen estate and are frequently tapped by accident during daily workflows. Because there is no toggle in settings to hide or disable these features, customers feel the software is prioritizing promotion over core usability. This intrusive layout change alienates long-standing supporters who rely on custom, distraction-free workspaces.

insights.recommendation: Add a user settings toggle to hide assistant search bars and restore quick-navigation shortcuts to the bottom toolbar.

insights.title: A severe text input bug causes the iPad screen to jump and hide active typing, rendering the mobile experience unusable.
insights.summary: Users utilizing iPads find themselves unable to see what they are typing because the display automatically scrolls or shifts downward away from the cursor. This defect breaks core note-taking capabilities and forces tablet users to abandon the mobile app entirely in favor of web or desktop alternatives. Despite repeated updates, the focus jump persists across multiple operating system versions, frustrating students and professionals alike. Without a fix for this layout behavior, the mobile app remains effectively broken for tablet owners.

insights.recommendation: Fix the view-adjustment logic on tablet interfaces so the cursor position remains vertically centered and visible while the system keyboard is active.
